name: Miracle Rise Chocolate Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Spray a 9-inch tube or Bundt pan with non-stick spray.
2. In a large mixing bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water. Let it sit for about 5 minutes until foamy.
3. Add egg substitute, milk, oil, and sweet liquid extract to the yeast mixture. Mix on medium speed until well combined.
4. Gradually add cocoa powder, flour (Pillsbury's All-Purpose Flour), baking powder, baking soda, salt, vanilla extract, and red food coloring to the batter. Mix on low speed just until no lumps remain.
5. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and cover with foil. Let it rise in a warm place for 45 minutes.
6. Remove foil and bake at 350°F (175°C) for 30-35 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
7. Invert the cake onto a wire rack to cool completely before serving.
Note: This recipe yields one large Miracle Rise Chocolate Cake.
